title: Pakoda (Onion Base)
time: 4–5 | Prep Time: 15 min | Cooking Time: 15 min
servings: Servings: 4–5 | Prep Time: 15 min | Cooking Time: 15 min

ingredients.title: Ingredients
items: 1 tbsp fresh mint leaves – chopped (optional, for freshness)
1 tsp red chili powder (adjust to taste)
½ tsp garam masala (optional for depth)
Water – about 3–5 tbsp (just enough to bind)

instruction.title: Prep Onions:
steps: Place sliced onions in a large mixing bowl. Sprinkle with a little salt, mix, and let sit for 5 minutes — this draws out moisture.

instruction.title: Build Flavor:
steps: Add green chili, coriander, mint, ajwain, cumin, turmeric, chili powder, and garam masala. Mix well.

instruction.title: Add Flours:
steps: Add gram flour and rice flour. Mix with your hands so the onion moisture starts binding with the flour.

instruction.title: Adjust Texture:
steps: Add water very sparingly (1 tbsp at a time). You want a sticky, clumpy mixture — not a runny batter.

instruction.title: Heat Oil:
steps: Heat oil in a kadhai/wok to 170–180°C (340–355°F). If you drop a small piece of batter, it should rise to the top in 3–4 seconds without burning.

instruction.title: Shape & Fry:
steps: Using your fingers, drop small, uneven clusters into the oil (don’t roll smooth balls — the rough texture gives more crisp edges). Fry in batches, turning occasionally, until golden brown (about 4–5 minutes).

instruction.title: Drain & Serve:
steps: Remove with a slotted spoon, drain on kitchen paper, and serve hot.

chefTips: Extra Crisp: A pinch of baking soda (just ⅛ tsp) added before frying can make pakodas even lighter.
Oil Temperature Control: Too hot = burnt outside, raw inside; too low = soggy pakodas.
Veggie Variations: Replace onions with shredded cabbage, grated potato, spinach, cauliflower florets, or mixed vegetables.
Serving Suggestion: Pair with mint-coriander chutney or tamarind chutney, plus hot masala chai for a true street-food vibe.
